taxonomy: Hieracium megalomastix is classified within the family Asteraceae and is a member of the genus Hieracium. The species is a type of hawkweed, a group of plants known for their yellow or white flowers. More specifically, Hieracium megalomastix is classified as a perennial herbaceous plant. At the rank of genus, it is a part of the subtribe Cichoriinae, which also includes other types of dandelions and hawkweeds. Overall, its classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Asteraceae family.

intro: Hieracium megalomastix, also known as St. John's Wort of the Alps, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family, characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, depression, and insomnia, due to its ability to regulate mood and improve sleep quality. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium megalomastix include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
